ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers soy milk as a food product; the cold/fresh serving variant is not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 covers soya milk as a substance; the fresh cold serving form is not separately coded. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of fresh cold soya milk is approximately 240 ml (one cup), as commonly served at Chinese breakfast stalls and Singaporean hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: Soymilk (soy beverage), unsweetened, with added calcium, vitamins A and D — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 16222 / FDC ID 174289)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy unsweetened soymilk used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent plain unsweetened soya milk per 100 g. The Din Tai Fung serving is described as fresh cold soya milk; no added sugar is assumed for the plain variant. Values adapted to reflect plain unsweetened fresh soya milk consistent with HPB Singapore nutrient data for soya bean milk (unsweetened).

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for soy milk, full-fat, unsweetened (GI=34)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 34 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for full-fat unsweetened soy milk. The fresh cold soya milk served at Din Tai Fung is plain and unsweetened, making this an appropriate analogous match. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (34 × 1.7 × 2.4) / 100 = 1.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (51 mg per 100 g); contains potassium (118 mg per 100 g) which supports blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: Fresh unsweetened soya milk is low in sodium and provides potassium, making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension; one cup (240 ml) contributes approximately 122 mg sodium, which is modest within a 2000 mg daily sodium limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 34) and low carbohydrate content (1.7 g per 100 g); provides plant protein which may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 34 and a glycemic load of approximately 1.4 per 240 ml serving, fresh unsweetened soya milk has a minimal impact on postprandial blood glucose and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.2 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and soy isoflavones with potential cardioprotective effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fresh unsweetened soya milk is low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free; regular consumption of soy protein has been associated with modest reductions in LDL cholesterol, supporting cardiovascular health when included as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fresh Soya Milk (Cold) — known in Chinese as 鲜豆浆（冷）(xiān dòujiāng lěng) — is a traditional Chinese plant-based beverage made by soaking dried soybeans, grinding them with water, and straining the resulting liquid. The cold variant is served chilled rather than hot, making it a refreshing option particularly popular in warm climates such as Singapore. At Din Tai Fung, it is offered as a premium freshly prepared beverage alongside dim sum and noodle dishes. The flavour is mild, slightly beany, and subtly sweet from the natural sugars in soybeans, with a smooth, thin-to-medium body. Nutritionally, unsweetened fresh soya milk provides approximately 33 kcal per 100 g, with 2.9 g protein, 1.8 g fat (predominantly polyunsaturated), and only 1.7 g carbohydrate, making it a low-calorie, low-glycaemic beverage. Its GI of 34 places it firmly in the low-GI category, and its glycaemic load per standard 240 ml serving is approximately 1.4. It is naturally dairy-free, vegan, and gluten-free, and is a source of plant protein, potassium, phosphorus, and B vitamins. Clinically, it is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its low sodium, low saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and low glycaemic profile. Soy isoflavones present in soya milk have been studied for potential benefits in LDL cholesterol reduction and bone health.
common_user_queries: Is soya milk good for diabetics?
How many calories in fresh soya milk?
Is Din Tai Fung soya milk sweetened?
What is the GI of soy milk?
Is soya milk good for blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for unsweetened soymilk, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for soya bean milk. GI of 34 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for full-fat unsweetened soy milk. The food is a well-characterised commodity beverage with consistent nutrient profiles across approved sources.
notes: trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; soya milk contains no trans fats by nature (naturally zero). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain unsweetened soymilk. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the plain unsweetened variant of the source database (fortified variants vary; this record represents unfortified fresh soya milk). vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as plain unfortified soya milk contains no vitamin B12; it is not reported in the source for the unfortified variant. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported for unfortified fresh soya milk in the source database. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this food. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this food. GI set to 34 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database analogous match for full-fat unsweetened soy milk. Serving size of 240 ml (approximately 240 g) used for GL calculation. The Din Tai Fung listing is for plain cold fresh soya milk; no added sugar is assumed. Price SGD 6.54 noted from field survey source but not included in nutrient record as it is not a nutritional field.
